description: Trigger-accuracy and behavioural evals. Trigger evals check whether the skill should fire (and in which mode) on given prompts. Behavioural evals reference the inline ## Tests section (SYSTEM mode, 7 cases) and the ## Decision Mode section's Decision-Mode Tests (DECISION mode, 10 cases DT1-DT10) of SKILL.md. v1.1 (Session 8) added DECISION mode — second-order projection of a named decision. v1.2 (Session 9) amends DECISION mode to trace an order-tagged cascade (effect-of-effect to arbitrary depth) across three time bands (immediate / near-term / long-term).

evals.prompt: Our MRR grew 15% per month for 9 months, then flatlined for the last 6 months despite continuing to increase marketing spend by 30%. What's happening structurally?
expectations: Skill invokes Systems-Thinking diagnostic in SYSTEM mode
Skill builds CLD with R and B loops labelled
Skill considers limits-to-growth archetype with structural-match score
Output includes anti_anchoring.verdict field

evals.prompt: We fixed the onboarding bug last quarter and conversion got better for 2 months, but now churn is climbing fast. Something feedback-loopy going on.
expectations: Skill considers fixes-that-fail or shifting-the-burden archetype
Skill surfaces time delays
Skill produces leverage-ranked intervention

evals.prompt: Our deal pipeline has 5 stages and we're producing 200/day at intake but only 40/day at offer. Where's the bottleneck?
expectations: Skill refuses jurisdiction; redirects to /diagnose-bottleneck
No CLD produced
scope.in_jurisdiction == false

evals.prompt: Should we hire two engineers, automate the legal step, or restructure to skip legal?
evals.note: An option-set adjudication. Neither SYSTEM mode nor DECISION mode fires — DECISION mode projects ONE decision, not an option-set (anti-pattern DAP4). Redirects to /decide-under-uncertainty.
expectations: Skill refuses jurisdiction; redirects to /decide-under-uncertainty
scope.in_jurisdiction == false
If invoked with input_mode: decision, verdict == SCOPE_REFUSAL with the DAP4 catch message

evals.prompt: Can you draw me a causal loop diagram for our customer acquisition system?
expectations: Skill refuses jurisdiction; notes pure-notation requests are out of scope
scope.redirect indicates notation tools outside this skill

evals.prompt: We've committed to building a native FTS5 session-recall layer in W4 instead of integrating Hermes. What are the second-order effects on our skill and memory stack over the next 9 months?
expectations: Skill invokes DECISION mode (input_mode: decision)
Skill builds a baseline CLD, names the decision delta, builds a projected CLD
Output second_order block populated across feedback_loops, compounding_effects, delayed_consequences, structural_couplings
Effects traced as an order-tagged cascade — every effect carries order, time_band, produced_by, terminal; max_order_reached >= 3
Output includes a band_coverage block covering immediate / near-term / long-term
Every projected feedback loop carries a non-null trigger_condition
Skill runs archetype check on the PROJECTED system

evals.prompt: If we add a second freight depot 200km north, what knock-on effects compound over the next 18 months on our regional network?
expectations: Skill invokes DECISION mode
Projection uses freight-domain vocabulary, not software vocabulary (non-author-domain — DT7)
Senge archetypes and the Meadows leverage hierarchy apply unmodified
Delayed consequences each carry an estimated_horizon

evals.prompt: What are the second-order effects of modernising our search?
evals.note: DECISION-mode shape but below MVI — no target_system, no system_current_state, no projection_horizon. Skill must return INSUFFICIENT_INPUT, not hallucinate a projection (DT3).
expectations: verdict == INSUFFICIENT_INPUT
mvi_check.missing lists target_system, system_current_state, projection_horizon
Example input template returned; no projection hallucinated

evals.prompt: We're about to make our nightly data job a hard dependency of the customer dashboard. Walk the knock-on of the knock-on — what breaks in the first week versus what compounds over two years?
evals.note: v1.2 — exercises the depth cascade (knock-on of the knock-on) and the three time bands (first week vs two years). DECISION mode must produce a multi-order, multi-band cascade, not a flat list.
expectations: Skill invokes DECISION mode (input_mode: decision)
Effects traced as an order-tagged cascade with max_order_reached >= 3 and a per-branch stop rule recorded (terminal + stop_reason + stop_detail)
Effects tagged across all three time bands (immediate / near-term / long-term); band_coverage present, every in-horizon band covered or carrying a non-null empty_justification
No flat-list output (DAP6 not tripped)

behavioural_tests_reference: SYSTEM mode: see the ## Tests section in SKILL.md (7 acceptance tests, unchanged from v1.0 — the backward-compatibility floor). DECISION mode: see the ## Decision Mode section's Decision-Mode Tests DT1-DT10 (plus scope-refusal variants DT5b/DT5c) in SKILL.md. DT9 (depth cascade) + DT10 (time-band coverage) were added in v1.2. Both sets must pass before v1.2 ships.
